reviews.review_text: even i got potato pc,this game can still run,love ts so much
Love devil may cry man def the best game and even better with mods gotta love that plastic chair

Devil May Cry 5 is pure stylish chaos. The combat is insanely smooth, every character feels unique, and the game honestly feels reborn every time you switch characters. Dante alone has more depth than most action games combined, and the game rewards skill instead of button mashing.

The soundtrack is legendary. Music ramps up with your combo rank, boss fights feel cinematic, and every cutscene somehow manages to be both goofy and unbelievably hype.

Optimization is amazing. The game runs buttery smooth even on older hardware while still looking better than most modern AAA games. Capcom cooked with the RE Engine.

The story can get confusing if you're new to DMC, but the gameplay carries hard. Once the combat clicks, it's impossible to put down.

10/10. Bury the Light deep within.

One of the best combat systems in any game ever. The music, the characters, and the mechanics are iconic beyond gaming itself. Vergil and Dante are some of the most iconic video game characters ever, and Bury the Light has been on constant repeat for me ever since I first heard it in 2020. SSS tier.

Devil May Cry 5 is an absolute masterpiece, and the soundtrack still gives me goosebumps. If you just want to play through the story and feel like a badass, it’s easily one of the best action games ever made. I’m incredibly proud to have this Platinum in my collection, but man, I am emotionally and physically exhausted. It proves I mastered the game, but Capcom really tested my limits.

Devil May Cry 5 is an absolute masterclass in character action gaming. It doesn’t just respect the legacy of the franchise, it elevates it to near perfection, delivering a stylish, adrenaline fueled spectacle that feels incredibly satisfying to play. Instead of sticking to one playstyle, the game gives you three entirely distinct characters. Nero uses his mechanical Devil Breaker arms to grapple and control space, Dante brings his classic, mind bogglingly deep style switching system, and V completely flips the script by playing like a tactical summoner who fights from the backline. Visually, the game is a stunning achievement. The minor flaws are the repetitive environments like the second half of the game takes place largely inside a giant, fleshy, underworld tree structure (The Qliphoth), leading to a lot of visually repetitive, gray and red corridors and also V's Playstyle isn't for everyone. But if you have any love for fast paced, mechanically deep action games, Devil May Cry 5 is an essential experience.
Honestly, a great game with nice and enjoyable gameplay and also a cool story. Whole franchise is epic.

[h1]😈🔥 “Stylish action perfected.” ⚔️🎸[/h1]

Devil May Cry 5 feels like the moment the series truly finds its stride. Everything — the combat, presentation, accessibility, and pacing — comes together into one of the smoothest action games Capcom has ever made.

The gameplay is incredibly polished. Combat feels fast, fluid, and responsive in a way that makes even basic encounters exciting. At the same time, it’s far more approachable than earlier entries in the series. There’s still a massive skill ceiling for players who want to master combos and style rankings, but the game does a much better job of making you feel cool right from the start.

Switching between Nero, Dante, and V keeps the campaign constantly fresh. Each character plays dramatically differently, offering completely unique approaches to combat. Nero feels aggressive and straightforward, Dante is endlessly versatile and chaotic, and V brings a more strategic, unconventional style that helps break up the pacing.

And visually? It’s stunning. The RE Engine absolutely shines here, delivering incredibly detailed characters, slick animations, and effects-heavy combat without sacrificing performance. The entire game oozes style from beginning to end.

The story leans hard into the series’ trademark bombastic energy, balancing ridiculous action with surprisingly heartfelt character moments. It fully embraces what Devil May Cry is supposed to be instead of trying to tone itself down.

Capcom clearly understood what worked and refined it into something special.

Flashy, accessible, mechanically deep, and ridiculously fun — this feels like the modern benchmark for stylish action games.

"Smokin' Sexy Style"

Did you ever dream of becoming the greatest motorcycle-poet-demonslayer in the world? DMC 5 is handing you that opportunity. Right now.

Alright, seriously. DMC 5 is a wonderful game for any gamer. The different difficulty levels let absolutely everyone feel like a professional death machine.

I have no idea what to say about the fight system here. This game just turned me inside out. It gives you a huge playground to discover your own SSS style.

The storyline here is quite interesting. The overall speed of the narrative and the regular flashbacks sure interfere a little with your understanding of the story. Along with the fact that in the whole chaos of what is happening in the world, you simply don't understand who is who.

Speaking of music - this game sounds like a rock opera from hell. Every battle theme is a shot of adrenaline straight to the heart. The screen is constantly screaming at you with letters, sparks, and explosions, and it's beautiful. It's pure chaos, but once you learn to dance in it, you become the chaos.

This isn't just a game. It's a mirror. It shows you exactly how cool you can be if you stop thinking and start doing.
__________________________

"Smokin' Sexy Style"

Ты когда-нибудь мечтал стать величайшим в мире мотоциклетно-поэтично-демоническим убийцей? DMC 5 дает тебе эту возможность. Прямо сейчас.

Ладно, если серьёзно. DMC 5 замечательная игра для любого геймера. Разные уровни сложности позволяют абсолютно каждому почувствовать себя профессиональной машиной для убийств.

Я даже не знаю, что сказать о боевой системе. Эта игра просто вывернула меня наизнанку. Она даёт огромное поле, чтобы раскрыть свой собственный SSS-стиль.

Сюжет здесь довольно интересный. Общая скорость повествования и постоянные флешбеки, конечно, немного мешают пониманию истории. Вкупе с тем фактом, что во всём хаосе происходящего в мире ты просто не понимаешь, кто есть кто.

Музыка - игра звучит как рок-опера из ада. Экран постоянно кричит на тебя буквами, искрами и взрывами, и это прекрасно. Это чистый хаос, но как только ты учишься в нём танцевать, ты сам становишься хаосом.

Это не просто игра. Это зеркало. Оно показывает тебе, насколько крутым ты можешь быть, если перестанешь думать и начнёшь делать.

10/10 🔥
Pehli baar DMC game khela aur pura enjoy kiya.
Combat bahut stylish hai, boss fights mast hai aur Vergil next level hai 😈
Story starting me confusing thi but ending bahut epic lagi
Good game, would recommend especially if you like to uphold religion... by killing demons
First pre-ordered DMCV back in 2019 and have accumulated over 300 hours in the game over the years. Still crazy how Capcom puts one of the best games of all time on sale for less than 5 bucks

Devil May Cry 5 delivers one of the best hack-and-slash experiences ever made with stylish combat, incredible action, and outstanding gameplay depth.

Pros:

+ Extremely satisfying and fast-paced combat system
+ Three playable characters with unique fighting styles
+ Stylish combo mechanics reward creativity and skill
+ Dante’s combat depth offers huge replay value
+ Smooth performance and impressive visuals using the RE Engine
+ Excellent soundtrack that dynamically reacts to gameplay
+ Memorable boss fights with cinematic presentation
+ Strong fan service for longtime Devil May Cry players
+ Responsive controls and polished gameplay mechanics
+ High replayability through difficulty modes and combo mastery

Cons:

- Story can feel confusing for newcomers to the series
- V’s gameplay style may not appeal to everyone

Despite a few minor flaws, it remains an incredibly fun and rewarding experience. Highly recommended for action game fans — and make sure to get Vergil, because his gameplay alone is worth it.

Devil May Cry 5 is an absolute blast from start to finish. As someone completely new to the series, I wasn’t sure what to expect — but within minutes I was hooked. The combat is fast, fluid, and ridiculously stylish, making every encounter feel like a chance to show off. Each character brings their own unique playstyle, and switching between them kept the whole experience fresh and exciting.

The story is surprisingly easy to follow even without playing the earlier games, and the cast is full of personality. The visuals are stunning, the soundtrack is electric, and the whole game oozes confidence and flair.

What impressed me most is how welcoming it is for newcomers. I never felt lost — just constantly entertained. I enjoyed it so much that I’m now planning to go back and play the previous entries in the franchise to see how it all began.

If you’re looking for stylish action, memorable characters, and pure fun, Devil May Cry 5 is absolutely worth your time.

Peak game, excellent story, character design, graphics, optimization, perfect combat system and the best music a video game can offer.
Overall 10/10
Came for the action, stayed for the graphics. Every combo looks insanely good and the game flows perfectly. 10/10 for style points

There’s something different about coming back to this game. It’s so perfect, and then you get the co-op mod and it’s just better. Wish I had my old console maybe I'm stuck in the past. legit just going through the games again, one day they gonna make a new one.
Never played any dmc game before but jesus h christ this game is just so damn good.  absolute w

Had fun playing DMC after a long time. The game's absolutely an SSS RANK.
- Story's great, quite enjoyable, bit comedic (especially when you see Dante adapting new skills).
- People say Vergil's an overkill character, but i don't see it, and requires some serious skills if you wanna enjoy playing with him. True that if you master him once and all easy in the later play.
- I would say Dante's a pure overkill. Once you farm DT through fights, its SDT is destructive!
- Amazing Graphics ofc.
- The thing annoyed me the most is "Difficulty Modes" LOL why made so many modes. Heaven or Hell is totally waste of time but still requires you to play it (It's basically a ONE HIT KILL MODE, unfortunately for you too, if an enemy hit you first then you are "FINISHED"). Also, Hell and Hell is nearly impossible, you get hit first, you done! but for the enemies are quite healthy in this mode). Somehow, I managed to complete it (REQUIRES REALLY SUPER SKILLS, TIMELY DODGING, AND CONCENTRATION).

- If we talk about Bloody Palace. It's a bit difficult as well.

According to my experience, I have finished this game many times on an other platform. 
100% Recommended!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
IF YOU LIKE SOME COMBO HACKING SLASHING, THEN YES IT'S TIME FOR YOU TO PLAY THIS AMAZING TITLE.
Fun, pretty hard in my book. Play on controller for better experience in my opinion.
one of the coolest games I've ever played, I love everything about it, especially the fighting mechanics. will definitely try to get all the achievements

And besides characters are treating each other like a piece of ♥♥♥♥. But there were really great parts which I found well written. This game has the cringiest one-liners in the history, and it is rly hilarious. So, what should I say? Nice, that would be my last word. Ez game

You can ride a motorcycle to attack and drift on your enemies as well as split it in half to keep the combo going. Its a bit slower paced for my liking but yeah, the hype is justified and the game's peak.

9/10

i also played the game on ps4
from one side, this game can feel and look incredible. from another... imo it can feel worse than dmc 3 in terms of impact of the weapons??? its weird. depends on moves and the characters

story is good for this kind of game

first run through is pretty awesome but then u want to start again on a higher difficulty...  and you still are locked to preset characters. this works on a first playthrough but on repeat ones i would like to have choice, like, for example, play the prologue as dante. and i cant. a big missed opportunity. it makes the game feel unsatisfyingly fragmented. it would make the replayability better. the only character that can play the whole game from the start to finish is the DLC vergil (and this aspect is good) tho he also has his own separate issues like his secret mission clears not unlocking HP upgrades like the OG campaign does. its nonsense

everyone knows the music, its nice tho tbh maybe i would want more variety with the battle themes. well, they are selling dlc for that i think..................... :/

the survival/arena mode, Bloody Palace, exists in this DMC too and its fun thru the sole virtue of it being... the arena mode but i think dmc 3 potentially did it better. here u have a time limit which so far hasnt been an issue at all, its easy to completely outpace it but kinda why is it even here, i dont think it contributes much. dmc 3 had no time limit and floors felt more procedural and progression between floors felt more varied 

the game has a fair amount of potential longevity in it, the additional difficulties and other unlocks u can get are cool

the locale design feels a bit bland. nothing jumps out much. design of the underworld is both interesting but also samey at the same time. maybe its the fact that you are in it for the vast majority of the game

back to combat
maybe a skill issue but i find Nero to be somewhat cumbersome to play, Exceed (timing "reloads" of ur sword between ur attacks, to put it simply) mechanic isnt exactly intuitive (the timings are all over the place) and without using it the visual feedback from attacks feels kinda ass (back to the point of the game feeling worse than dmc3 at some points). he has devil breakers, grabs, and an "overdrive" mode but i feel like his core moveset is just kinda limiting.... like his sword maybe isnt interesting enough? im not sure, i might need to play him more. i feel like giving him a second weapon could do a lot to him, and yea devil breakers exist and they kinda cool but idk man..... i would complain less about him if the game didnt force me to play him. and dont get me wrong, hes still cool, lol, but i also wish he was... cooler. but also again, maybe my skill issue.... i need to get better at SSSing with him, thats my problem

bosses have some interesting ideas. i would want to have them available in the training mode to learn them better but i cant. u can force spawn them in the Bloody Palace mode but u can only do that AFTER u beat them in that mode, and its also on per character basis. so its not optimal.

v and dante are cool, so is vergil. dante has some cool pretty ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ weapons and V completely changes the rhythm of the gameplay, can make it feel like an RTS action game hybrid lol. and its fun. and vergil is vergil

all in all this is a very impressive game, all these faults be damned. worth checking out, the combat has a lot of depth

downloaded on sale for 5$ because of season 2 of devil may cry on netflix. u can use a freaking motorcycle as a weapon. I would have paid full price for this game buy the deluxe edition of the game, buy the dvd version of the game on the PS5, Xbox 360, Xbox one which i dont even have one, buy minifigures of dante, and buy the ultimate most expensive version of netflix possible just to express my extreme gratitude of this game. but i just can't afford any of that stuff

I have pretty mixed feelings about DMC 5. For most of the game, I was enjoying the combat and the stylish presentation, but at the same time I was getting tired of how repetitive everything felt.
The combat is undeniably cool. Pulling off combos, switching weapons, and destroying enemies feels damm good. The cutscenes are also W. The music is good throughout the game, but just like the story, it really comes alive near the end.

My biggest problem with DMC5 is that a lot of the campaign follows the same pattern. You fight through waves of normal enemies, reach the end of a mission, beat a fairly easy boss, and then do it all over again. After a while, it started feeling repetitive enough that I even thought about dropping the game.
But the last few missions completely changed my opinion of the game. The boss fights suddenly become much more exciting, the story starts hitting harder, and everything feels like it's building toward something meaningful. The final bosses are easily the best fights in the game .
And those last two missions? Pure cinema. The combat, the music, the cutscenes, the story everything was perfect. 
Honestly, if the game had maintained the quality of its final few missions throughout the entire campaign, it could have been a W game for me. Most of the game sits around  5/10 for me , but the ending is so strong that it pushes my overall score up quite a bit.
Final Rating: 7/10

De los mejores hack and Slash, tremenda banda sonora, la historia 10/10 en conclusion un Juegaso.

A legendary hack and slash game about 3 dudes looking very cool. The style is the main appeal and something to be remembered; it speaks for itself and earning an S rank really feels good. The gameplay is good though many missions don't give you long enough combat segments to develop skills and there is an overabundance of cutscenes. It's not a flawless PC port but also not terrible.
The main caveat is trying to get mission S ranks, which has me cursing the dev's bloodlines on multiple occasions; they must have had an intern write some of these values. On the other hand, it really shows how much better ULTRAKILL's ranking system is.

After playing and finishing DMC, I can say one thing: this game is definitely not for everyone. In my opinion, the controls feel very outdated, and the camera system can be frustrating. It constantly locks onto bosses or enemies and doesn't let you properly see what's happening around you, making it harder to adjust your strategy, retreat, or attack when you're low on health.
I will still give it a positive review, but only because the story and characters are genuinely interesting and worth experiencing. Unfortunately, the gameplay leaves a lot to be desired.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Погравши та пройшовши DMC, можу сказати лише одне: ця гра явно підійде не кожному. На мою думку, у грі дуже застаріле керування та поведінка камери. Вона постійно фіксується на босі або противнику й не дає нормально оцінити обстановку навколо, щоб скоригувати свої дії та тактику для відступу чи атаки, особливо коли закінчуються очки здоров'я.

Я все ж поставлю «Рекомендую», але лише тому, що сюжет і персонажі тут дійсно цікаві та варті уваги. Проте геймплей, на мою думку, залишає бажати кращого.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

DMCをプレイしてクリアした後に言えることは一つです。このゲームは間違いなく万人向けではありません。

個人的には、操作性がかなり古く感じられ、カメラワークにも不満があります。カメラが常にボスや敵に固定されるため、周囲の状況を把握しにくく、特に体力が少ないときに攻撃や撤退の判断をするのが難しく感じました。

それでも私は「おすすめ」を付けます。なぜなら、ストーリーとキャラクターは本当に魅力的で、体験する価値があるからです。ただし、ゲームプレイについては改善の余地があると感じました。

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Поиграв и пройдя DMC, могу сказать только одно: эта игра явно подойдёт не каждому. На мой взгляд, в игре очень устаревшее управление и работа камеры. Она постоянно фиксируется на боссе или противнике и не позволяет нормально оценить ситуацию вокруг, чтобы скорректировать свои действия и тактику для отступления или атаки, особенно когда остаётся мало здоровья.

Я всё же поставлю игре «Рекомендую», но только потому, что сюжет и персонажи здесь действительно интересные и заслуживают внимания. Однако геймплей, на мой взгляд, оставляет желать лучшего.

Devil May Cry 5 is widely considered one of the best action games ever made and the strongest entry in the series since Devil May Cry 3. It successfully modernized the franchise while keeping the fast, stylish combat that fans love. 
If you like action games, Devil May Cry 5 is one of the safest recommendations in gaming. Its combat system is deep enough for hundreds of hours of mastery, yet accessible enough for newcomers to enjoy from the first mission. The story and level design aren't perfect, but the combat alone places it among the best action games ever made.

Devil May Cry 5

Gameplay - 10/10
- The gameplay is amazing and is truly the peak of hack and slash.
Fast paced, 4 characters, (Nero, V, Dante, Vergil DLC) 
Soundtrack - 10/10
- Bury the light/Devil trigger
Story - 9/10
- The story was interesting but fairly simple. 
Characters - 9/10
- All the characters are amazing
- Vergil
Difficulty - 4/10
- Initial difficulty (Human - Devil Hunter) is kind of easy, because of
gold orbs, (You get literally more than 8) and the option to revive with 
red orbs.
- Difficulty spikes alot when you choose to go up to harder difficulties (Son Of Sparda - Dante Must Die)
and the gold orbs and the red orbs might go away too because you will struggle alot and will need to revive
DLC - 10000/10
- Vergil


Overall - 11/10 
- Amazing hack and slash masterpiece and is truly the PEAKEST PEAK PEAK of its genre. It ticks all the good checks.
Vergil may cry is a game about Vergil hiring Vergil's brother and Vergil's son to defeat Vergil so Vergil could become Vergil, featuring Vergil, special Vergil edition.

Devil May Cry 5 is a really nice slashing game. The combat feels fast, stylish, and satisfying, and honestly, me likey.

The game has a great flow once you get into it. Pulling off combos, switching between attacks, and destroying enemies feels really fun. It has that over-the-top action style that makes every fight feel cool.

The visuals are solid, the characters are entertaining, and the gameplay has a lot of energy. It is not a game I take too seriously, but that is part of the charm. You jump in, slash everything, look cool doing it, and have a good time.

Overall, Devil May Cry 5 is a fun, stylish, and satisfying action game. If you enjoy fast combat and flashy sword-slashing gameplay, it is definitely worth playing. Me likey.

fixed nero gameplay from 4 with devil breakers but gave the one dimensional combat to V 
3.5/5

This is my first time playing a Devil May Cry game. It was on sale, so I thought I should give it a try. First of all, I have a potato laptop and yet this game is working just fine.🥹 I'm really glad I gave this game a chance. Beautiful graphics + music. So many cool combos to learn and really cool fight scenes. 

Also, I feel like the amount of red orbs you get to upgrade your character is just enough after every mission. I didn't feel like "Gosh, I need to fight more to get this or that." It's more like, "I'll get this and this and this now, and then in the next mission, I'll get this one and this one and this one too." I just really hope this game won't end up feeling like a grindy game later on. 🫠

One last thing, I just want to say I have trypophobia and there are certain angles in this game that are very triggering. Playing this game is such a struggle for me, I'd be pressing the keys, holding my mouse, and then I'll see it and I'll cover my eyes, and then I can hear Vergil being attacked so i will fight back, and then I'll see another angle and I will have to look away again. Repeat. 😭

Honestly, the amount of effort I am giving just to be able to play this game should be enough to convince any newbie out there to give this game a chance too. 🤧

Even though the Devil May Cry series has always had over-the-top story elements and flashy gameplay, the only possible way to describe DMC5 is that it [i]literally touches itself[/i] in front of its audience for the entire duration of the game. Of course, it does this [i]unashamedly.[/i]

A demonic tree from hell itself bursts up through the ground, placing the city of Red Grave (and the entire world) at risk. With the ultimate goal of being stylish, and a secondary objective of saving the world, the player will fight from outside out of the tree and work their way down, slaying anything that lies in their path. Seated upon a throne, a newly born demon lord waits for the tree to fruit and give him the power he so desperately has been seeking. Over twenty missions, you'll control three separate characters to hack-and-slash your way to victory over hell once again.

The Devil Hunter, Nero, following up from the previous DMC game, fights with his signature scrappy, kit-bash style. For Nero, style always comes second to youthful overconfidence. The Red Queen sword and Blue Rose pistol compliment themselves just as well as DMC4, and Exceed remains one of the most satisfying aspects of Nero's arsenal. With the help of his new mechanic friend Nico, he'll make up for losing that Devil Bringer power that made players fall in love with his character in the first place. While the developers provided a lot of options for his new primary combat mechanic, I found most of them to be lackluster. Nero truly shines with his endgame upgrades on campaign replays, if that's your kind of thing.

The Enigma, V, is a puzzling character for most of the game. The summon-weaken-finish mechanic for his combat is unintuitive to start, but becomes addicting after the first few V-focused missions. As you recite poetry along the edge of the battlefield, you command your minions to crush, slash, and stab your enemies beneath a cloak of shadow. Upgrading V's abilities is the key to making his character enjoyable to play in combat, especially when it comes to late-game. For veteran DMC1 players, the creatures V calls to his aid should be reminiscent [i]for better or for worse.[/i]

The Legendary Devil Hunter, Dante, is more complex than he has ever been. Not only does he have four distinct styles and many questionably-effective weapons, each of those styles and weapons have variations and upgrades that fundamentally change their gameplay capabilities. Half of Dante's gameplay is trying to figure out how the hell to execute a specific move, and the other half is accidentally using the wrong move but it ends up working anyway. I believe the developers truly abided by the policy of [b][i]"Random ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ go!!!!"[/i][/b] when it comes to Dante's kit. If you're good at not fumbling your fingers mid-combat and you're great at fighting game combo-moves, you'll love this iteration of our cringey, boy-fail protagonist.

While most evil lurks in the shadows, this one aura farms in broad daylight. Vergil is as powerful as he is infamous, and will familiarize you with the taste of dirt if you do not give him the respect he commands. Beating Vergil in DMC5 is a monumental challenge, generally requiring full use of Dante's four fighting styles and extensive arsenal. It truly brings me back to DMC3, fighting the same power-hungry, white-haired loser for hours on end. Every time you fight Vergil, it feels like you're in a thirst trap edit of some second-rate anime character. You know it's cringe... [i]but you just can't bring yourself to look away.[/i]

While the story isn't exactly blockbuster, you'll enjoy seeing the development of the characters and their interactions as circumstances turn from bad to worse. I very much enjoyed Nico's role in the story as a supporting character, and found it interesting how they utilized Lady and Trish within the game's narrative. However, most of the backstory behind these characters is left nebulous outside of basic logs provided by Nico post-game. As per my DMC4 review, I truly believe DMC4 is the best starting point for new players! Understanding Nero and his ambitions will greatly assist you in enjoying DMC5 and all of the content it has to offer. If you really want the optimal experience, playing DMC4 and then DMC3 will set you up near-perfectly for anything this game will throw at you. 

Either way, Devil May Cry 5 is a great experience that's worth sinking your teeth into, especially if you already love some of these beloved characters. Never did I think I'd watch Dante hit a Michael Jackson dance sequence, but here we are.

[b]DMC5:[/b] You might get your arm ripped off by a dimension-splitting deadbeat dad, but don't worry. It'll grow back.
Wow. This game. It was beautiful. I beat it, and wowzers. The combat, the story. Everything is so fantastic.

[h1] Solid 7/10 [/h1]

I waited 7 years to play this one and yeah I'll admit, it's pretty good.

The writing in this game is in this awkward place between way too heavy handed and pretty good. So much so that it can really sour some moments that would hit a lot harder if the characters were just a little more subtle. Trish going "I should know because I'm a demon!"  in a conversation between two people that could already make that assumption was just a bit much. However whenever Nico is on screen all those fears are null and void since she's the best character in this series since DMC3 Virgil. The plot is interesting and feels rewarding for people that have played the entries before without letting itself get too key-jingly, despite half the game being references to DMC1 it felt tasteful. The plot serves as a sort of character dissection for Virgil and I found what they do with "V" to be entertaining. Gameplay is pretty good but just a tad too floaty for my taste. It's very much built for combo demons and that's not inherently a bad thing seeing as this is the combo demon video game. Music is good, this game gave us some pretty iconic themes so I don't have much to harp on other than wishing it had more memorable and atmospheric normal level music, a lot of it was just generic background music. 

Solid entry I can't ask for much more. I still tend to prefer DMC4 but this game left me wanting a DMC6, which is what any entry in a long running series should strive to do.

[h1]Over-the-Top Cheese in a Flesh Nightmare[/h1]

Devil May Cry 5 is a remarkably stylish action game that manages to shine despite some structural and mechanical friction. Spectacle fighters are not typically my primary genre, but the fact that I blew right past Steam’s two-hour refund window and kept pushing through speaks volumes about its core appeal. It has an infectious energy that hooks you, even when its mechanics and environments try their best to push back.

[hr][/hr]

[b]Campy Glory vs. Netflix Whiplash[/b]
The best part is its unapologetic ridiculousness. It fully embraces camp, think Ash from Evil Dead, with over-the-top characters, cheesy one-liners, and pure swagger.

The story is genuinely fun and captures that perfect cheesy Devil May Cry energy. Unfortunately, I complicated things for myself by watching the Netflix anime in between missions. Since the two are set in the same universe, the small but noticeable differences in lore and characterization made jumping back into the game feel oddly disorienting.

[b]Stunning Soundtrack, Exhausting Visuals[/b]
Where the game stumbles hardest is visual variety. Later chapters devolve into a mind-numbing slog of repetitive, monochromatic corridors drenched in red flesh, black voids, grey blood vessels, and sickly browns and yellows. Searching for hidden items becomes genuinely fatiguing on the eyes.

Fortunately, the killer soundtrack steps in to carry the momentum. The audio team completely understood the assignment, delivering a high-octane score that perfectly matches the frantic pacing of the combat and single-handedly salvages the dull, organic hallways you are forced to run through.

[b]Character Rotation Breaks the Flow[/b]
The combat styles are refreshingly distinct across Nero, Dante, and V. However, the campaign constantly rips control away from you just as you settle into a rhythm, handing you an entirely different playstyle.

Dante is the most technical of the three but oddly lacks Nero’s raw mobility, even with Trickster stance. Nero’s arm system is especially frustrating — you can’t preview or swap new arms without destroying the current one, so I mostly ignored their unique abilities. V’s summon-based playstyle is excellent and surprisingly deep, but he feels underused.

[b]Finicky Movement & Cinematic Camera[/b]
The movement and defensive options have noticeable quirks. Dodges are relative to the camera rather than your character, so the constant cinematic camera sweeps often make evasion unreliable exactly when you need it. Jumps and platforming also feel rigid and overly precise.

[b]Lazy PC Port[/b]
These issues are made worse by a very console-first PC port. Keyboard and mouse controls feel like an afterthought, and the tutorial tips display mixed keyboard & controller prompts for the entire game.

[hr][/hr]

[b]Final Verdict[/b]
Ultimately, Devil May Cry 5 is a fantastic, high-energy ride that succeeds on the sheer strength of its charm and combat variety, even if it forces you to deal with a tedious art style and an unpolished PC port. 

If a spectacle fighter can keep someone who doesn't even care for the genre playing well past the refund window, it should be considered a massive recommendation for anyone who actually loves this style of game. Just make sure to plug in a controller to bypass the lazy keyboard mapping.
